steps.title: Identify the core finding
steps.content: The researchers found that hedonic motivation—how pleasant users perceive the premium service to be—positively influences their intention to adopt it.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: Choice A proposes that companies should enhance how pleasant users perceive the premium service. This directly follows from the finding that hedonic motivation positively influences adoption intentions. B makes an unsupported service-specific loyalty claim; C contradicts the finding by questioning measurement reliability; D overstates the result as the only driver of adoption. That matches choice A — A is correct.
